O. 548(E).-Whereas the Govemment of India had set up an Expen Committee, under the Insecticides Act, 1966 (46 of l96E), with a view to investigate the continued use of cerhin insecticides i.e. Aldrin, Heptachlor and Chlordane in India, which were banned in otber court-ies because of involved risk to hrman beings and animals, as to render it expedient or necessary to take immediaE action; And whereas thc Certral Govemment duly considered the recommendations made by the said Commiree and was satisfied that the continued use of Aldrin, Chlordane and Heptachlor is likely to involve health hazards to human beings and animals; And whereas in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (2) of section 27 of he Insecticides Acl 1968, a notification was issued by the Governnent oflndia underthe Minisu-y ofAgriculture @epartnent ofAgriculture and Coopera- tion) vide number S, O. 138(E), dated 17 -2-1992, banning the Aldrin with effect fiom l - 1-1994 ard banning tbe use of both Chlordane and Heptachlor with effect from 17-2-1992; And wheresas I\tlls. Pesticides lndia Limited filed a wit petition challenging dre said notification in High Court of Rajasthan and the said High Court gave judgement on 17-3-1993 ther tho said notification was issued without following the principles ofnaoraljustice and without affording an opportunity ofbehg heard to fte regisEation holders and quashed the said notification with a direction to reronsider the matter as r€gards total ban on sale, productiou, formulation or import of Hep tachlor and Chlordane in India after affording an oppomuity ofhearing 0o the petitioners and other registration holders ofthese two hsecticides; - And whereas the Registtion Comminee constihled under section 5 ofthe Ilsecticides Act, 196E, in order'o give due compliance to the order ofthe Honourabte High Courq gavc adequate opportutrity to he said p€titioDers and otter registratior holders and also after convening several meetings with the industry, registration holders and deliberations in an inter-Ministerial meeting, he said Committee made certain recommendations to the Governmeot of India; Now, therefore, in exercise ofthe powers confened by sub-section (2) ofsection 27 ofthe Insecticides Ac! t968, the Central Governnent, aft€r considerhg the recomEeDdation ofthe Regisu'ation Committee, and on being satisfied tbaf the use of Aldrin, Chlordane and Heptachlor is likely to cause risk to hrunan beingi ald anima.ls, hereby makes the following order, namely :- ( I ) the manufactue, import and use of Heptachlor and Ctlordane shall be prohibited in India with immediate effecl; @ the certificate of registration issued by the Registration Conmitt€e to various persons under section 9 of 6e said insecticides shall stand canc€lled and the validity offte menu:flacan'ing licences issued to them by he Licencing Officer shall also sand cancelled; (3) the use of Aldrin shall be prohibited in India and the certificate of regisnation issued under the said Act shall statrd cancelled.
